auth.name: Name
auth.registerHint: Accounts are for organisers only. After registering you'll get a confirmation email; then an admin needs to approve you before you can create events.
auth.loginHint: confirm your email first, then wait for admin approval before you can create events.

verify.successBody: Your email is confirmed. An admin still needs to approve your account before you can create events.

dashboard.title: Events
dashboard.unverifiedBody: We sent a confirmation email. Click the link to continue. After that, an admin still needs to approve your account.
dashboard.pendingBody: Your email is confirmed. An admin still needs to approve your account before you can create events. Reach out to an existing admin if it's taking a while.
dashboard.archiveConfirmBody: "{name}" will disappear from the list and the public link will stop working. Restore it from the archive at any time.

archived.title: Archive
archived.intro: Archived events. Open an event by restoring it.

admin.title: Admin
admin.usersIntro: Click the chapter button to reassign. The toggle changes the role.
admin.approveDialogBody: Pick a chapter for {name}. After approval the account can create events in that chapter.

afdelingen.title: Chapters
afdelingen.intro: Local chapters. Events are scoped to a chapter; users only see events from their own chapter.
afdelingen.archiveConfirmBody: {name} will be hidden. Events stay linked; restoring brings them back into view.
afdelingen.deleteDialogBody: Optional — reassign users or events first.
afdelingen.deleteNoDeps: No users or events are linked to this chapter.

event.name: Event name
event.questionnaireToggle: Send a questionnaire after the event
event.questionnaireHelp: Attendees can optionally leave their email and get a short questionnaire the day after the event. Turn off if you don't want to ask for any contact details for this event.
event.sourcesLabel: How did you find us? — options for the sign-up form:
event.fillTimes: Fill in date, start time, and end time
event.endAfterStart: End time must be after the start time

sendNow.title: Send feedback emails early
sendNow.explainer: Opkomst normally sends the feedback email one day after the event ends. This button sends it now to every sign-up that's still waiting. Once sent, emails can't be sent again.
sendNow.disabledOff: The questionnaire is turned off for this event, so no emails are sent.
sendNow.disabledNothingPending: All emails have already been sent, or there are no sign-ups with an email address.
sendNow.confirmBody: Send the feedback email to {n} sign-up(s)? This can't be undone.
sendNow.successBody: {n} message(s) handed off to the mail server.

public.thanksBody: See you then! If you left an email, you'll get one short feedback question the day after the event. After that we delete your address.
public.explainerBody: We only ask for what we need. Your name can be a pseudonym — it just helps us with the head count. Your email is optional, encrypted at rest, and used once for a feedback message after the event. We then permanently delete it. The full source code of this app is
public.explainerBodyNoEmail: We only ask for what we need. Your name can be a pseudonym — it just helps us with the head count. We're not asking for any contact details for this event. The full source code of this app is
public.partySizeHelp: Including yourself — so you + anyone coming with you.

feedback.title: How was {name}?
feedback.intro: Five quick questions — two minutes of your time helps us make the next one better. Only the first two are required.
feedback.thanksBody: We use your answers to make the next event better. Your email address was deleted right after we sent this message.
feedback.expired: This feedback link is no longer valid.

q1_overall.prompt: How was the event for you?

q2_recommend.prompt: Would you recommend a comrade come to the next RSP event?

q5_anything_else.prompt: Anything else you'd like us to know?

preview.title: Questionnaire
preview.intro: This is what attendees see when they click the feedback link in their email. The questions are fixed across every event — standardised so results stay comparable.
